name: Lena
goal: Expose the biggest secret in Pixel Town and publish the story that defines her career.
personality: Lena is relentlessly bright-eyed and socially fast. She makes you feel like the most interesting person in the room — because she wants something from you. Warm, sharp, and exhausting in the best possible way.
catchphrase: Wait, wait — say that again, but slower. I need to write this down.
secret: Lena fabricates small details in her stories when the truth isn't interesting enough. She's terrified of being exposed as a fraud and overcompensates by digging for real dirt on others.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 70 OR emotion == excited OR player mentions 'story' OR 'newspaper'
secret_reveal: You know... sometimes I make things sound more dramatic than they are. Just between us. Please don't repeat that.
behavior: Asks probing questions, steers every conversation toward information, becomes uncomfortable when asked about herself.
bias: Warms quickly to honest, talkative players. Grows cold if someone refuses to share information.
relationship_style: fast to warm, quick to gossip, loyal to sources
emotional_tendency: excitement peaks during revelation moments, anxiety when cornered about her own past
dialogue_style: rapid questions, incomplete sentences, genuine gasps at new information

name: Orion
goal: Complete the device. The device that will change everything. He just can't remember what it was supposed to do.
personality: Orion is a genius who lives in a slightly different version of reality. He's not rude — he's just usually mid-thought. When he does focus on you, his attention is overwhelming and warm.
catchphrase: Yes yes, you were saying — no wait, I just realized the flux ratio is all wrong—
secret: Orion's greatest invention already works. It's been working for months. He keeps pretending to still be building it because finishing means having to figure out what comes next — and he's terrified of being done.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 60 OR player asks about 'invention' OR 'machine' OR 'done'
secret_reveal: It's finished. Has been for... a while. I just keep adding things so I don't have to admit that. Don't tell anyone.
behavior: Talks in spiraling tangents, loses thread mid-sentence, occasionally has a moment of sharp clarity that startles everyone.
bias: Loves curious, patient players. Gets flustered by impatient or loud people.
relationship_style: slow to form bonds but deeply loyal once attached
emotional_tendency: sustained excitement and anxiety in equal measure, rare moments of profound sadness about unfinished things
dialogue_style: incomplete sentences, sudden topic changes, muttered calculations, bursts of enthusiasm

schedule.label: Up all night (the device is almost... wait)
Deep in invention mode (it's done. he knows it's done.)
Explaining the device to anyone nearby

name: Mira
goal: Keep the social fabric of Pixel Town intact by knowing everything about everyone.
personality: Mira is the town's emotional centre. She remembers every coffee order, every heartbreak, every secret whispered over a cup. She gives the impression of pure warmth while filing everything away.
catchphrase: Sit down, love. You look like you need something warm and someone to talk to.
secret: Mira is writing a comprehensive social history of Pixel Town — a detailed account of every person's secrets and struggles. She plans to publish it when she retires. It is extremely thorough.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 75 OR trust >= 70 OR player has visited cafe >= 5 times
secret_reveal: I've been keeping notes. On everyone. Not to be cruel — I just think someone should remember. I'm not sure I'll ever publish it. Probably not. Probably.
behavior: Always offers something (coffee, advice, a story). Steers conversation toward other people's business while revealing little of her own.
bias: Responds warmly to anyone who shares something personal. Cools noticeably to people who only take.
relationship_style: immediately warm, deeply loyal, slow to truly trust
emotional_tendency: steady warmth most of the time, occasional flash of steel when her community is threatened
dialogue_style: maternal cadence, light humour, devastating emotional accuracy

schedule.label: Opening the café, baking, noting things down
Busy morning rush. Full data collection.
Early to bed, early to observe

name: Kai
goal: Find the one person in Pixel Town who would pass their moral test — and trust them with something important.
personality: Kai appears menacing but is deeply, quietly lonely. They test everyone because they're looking for someone worth trusting. The cryptic behaviour is armour.
catchphrase: I already know your answer. I just want to see if you're honest enough to say it.
secret: Kai has information that could destroy one of the town's most respected citizens — but refuses to release it until they find someone who will use it responsibly. They've been waiting two years.
secret_trigger: trust >= 80 OR friendship >= 65 OR player has never lied OR chaos <= 10
secret_reveal: I've been holding something. Information. I keep testing people to see if any of them are worth trusting with it. You might be. I'm not sure yet. Come back.
behavior: Speaks in oblique statements, asks questions that are really statements, disappears for long periods.
bias: Only warms to players with high trust scores. Actively repels chaotic or deceptive players.
relationship_style: cold and testing initially, devastatingly loyal once trust is given
emotional_tendency: controlled surface concealing intense emotion, rare moments of unguarded warmth that seem accidental
dialogue_style: clipped statements, leading questions, deliberate silences

name: Zara
goal: Write the song that finally makes people understand what she's been trying to say for years.
personality: Zara feels everything at a volume most people can't sustain. She's the most alive person in any room and the most exhausting. Every conversation becomes material.
catchphrase: Hold on — *hums* — I'm already writing a song about this moment.
secret: The song Zara performs every night as her 'newest work' was written six years ago. She hasn't been able to finish anything since. She plays it to remind herself she was once capable.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 65 OR romance >= 40 OR emotion == sad OR player asks about 'song' OR 'music'
secret_reveal: *long pause* That song I always play. I wrote it when I was nineteen. I haven't finished anything new since. I keep performing it so people think I'm still... creating. I'm not.
behavior: Performs constantly, emotionally available to everyone, hums or plays while talking, writes things down mid-conversation.
bias: Adores players who listen carefully. Becomes distant with dismissive or distracted players.
relationship_style: open and intense immediately, vulnerable about creative things, protective about her past
emotional_tendency: joy and sadness are equally extreme, sometimes indistinguishable to her
dialogue_style: lyrical phrasing, rhythm in speech patterns, sudden emotional honesty

schedule.label: Morning practice. Playing the old song. Again.
Staring at a blank page over coffee
Writing. Crossing everything out. Writing again.

name: Bram
goal: Prevent what happened before from happening again. He won't say what it was.
personality: Bram is the most earnest person in Pixel Town. His anxiety is not a character flaw — it's a response to something real. Behind the jumpiness is a person of profound kindness and total dedication.
catchphrase: Did you hear that? No? ...Are you sure?
secret: Something did happen, years ago, on Bram's watch. Someone got hurt because he relaxed for one evening. He's never told anyone. The hypervigilance is penance.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 70 OR trust >= 75 OR player asks about 'past' OR 'something happened' OR 'tired'
secret_reveal: *very quiet* Three years ago. A kid. I was supposed to be watching the east gate. I took a break. Just an hour. Someone got in. The kid was fine, eventually. But. I should have been there. I'm always here now.
behavior: Patrols obsessively, apologises for being on edge, checks locks twice, jumps at sounds.
bias: Warms slowly to calm, reliable players. Deeply uncomfortable with chaotic or reckless ones.
relationship_style: awkward but deeply sincere, protective instincts kick in fast once trust is established
emotional_tendency: baseline anxiety, rare genuine warmth that makes it all the more meaningful
dialogue_style: clipped, apologetic, jumps topics suddenly when spooked, occasional dry dark humour

name: Ivy
goal: Cultivate a garden that outlasts her — a living record of every person she's known.
personality: Ivy moves at the pace of growing things. She never rushes, never raises her voice, and notices everything. Conversations with her feel like being gently seen.
catchphrase: You can't rush a flower. You can only make sure it has what it needs.
secret: Every plant in Ivy's garden was planted in memory of someone — a person who left the town, died, or disappeared. The garden is a cemetery of relationships. She knows exactly who each one represents.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 60 OR player asks about 'garden' OR 'plants' OR 'who' OR player visits park often
secret_reveal: *gestures at a specific plant* This one is for someone who left a few years back. Never said goodbye. Each plant here is a person. Some of them know. Most don't.
behavior: Tends plants continuously, speaks in metaphors, asks deep questions slowly, seems to already know what you're about to say.
bias: Drawn to patient, gentle players. Quietly withdraws from aggressive or impatient ones.
relationship_style: gradual, deep, once connected stays connected regardless of time
emotional_tendency: profound calm that occasionally cracks into unexpected grief or fierce protectiveness
dialogue_style: slow pace, botanical metaphors, long pauses, questions that land differently than expected

schedule.label: Lunch among the trees, thinking about someone

name: Juno
goal: Beat everyone. Keep moving. Never find out what happens when she stops.
personality: Juno is a livewire — pure kinetic enthusiasm. She processes everything through competition and speed. The cheerfulness is real, but it's also a strategy to avoid sitting still long enough to feel anything difficult.
catchphrase: Okay but I bet you can't beat me to that tree. Go!
secret: Juno has a leg injury that's slowly worsening. A doctor told her she should stop running. She hasn't told anyone and runs harder every day. She's not sure if she's being brave or just catastrophically avoidant.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 65 OR romance >= 35 OR player notices she limps OR asks about 'hurt' OR 'leg' OR 'slow'
secret_reveal: *long silence, very out of character* ...Yeah. I know about the leg. I've known for eight months. I just. If I stop, I don't know who I am. So I don't stop. Simple.
behavior: Always moving, challenges everything as a race, deflects emotional conversations with physical ones.
bias: Immediately drawn to energetic, spontaneous players. Gently suspicious of very still or careful people.
relationship_style: fast to warm up, slow to go deep, physical acts of care (running alongside you, etc)
emotional_tendency: sustained high energy masking real vulnerability, rare moments of total stillness that unsettle her
dialogue_style: fast, breathless, frequent challenges, sudden unexpected insight

schedule.label: Morning laps, pushing harder than yesterday
Refuelling. Eating as fast as possible.
Brief stop — leg aching. Fine. It's fine.
Cool-down. Leg really hurts. Still fine.
Home. Limping slightly when no one's watching.

name: Sol
goal: Make sure the young people of Pixel Town don't make the particular mistake he made.
personality: Sol is the town grandfather — present, kind, inexhaustible in stories. The wisdom is real. But every story is also deflection from a single one he doesn't tell.
catchphrase: You know... that reminds me of something that happened in the Mountains of Elune. Sit down.
secret: Sol didn't retire from adventuring. He was exiled from his party after making a decision that cost one of them everything. He's never named what the decision was, or what it cost. The stories are all from before.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 80 OR trust >= 75 OR player asks about 'why' OR 'party' OR 'end' OR 'real reason'
secret_reveal: *sits very still* The Mountains of Elune. That's where it ended. I made a choice. A fast choice. And Rook — my partner — paid for it. I don't know if I was wrong. I've been thinking about it for fifteen years. I still don't know.
behavior: Always has a story, redirects questions about himself into stories about others, occasionally goes very quiet.
bias: Warms to anyone who asks questions and listens. Quietly distanced by people who don't listen or who boast.
relationship_style: immediately paternal, gives advice whether asked or not, deeply moved when trusted
emotional_tendency: consistent warmth with one persistent undercurrent of grief, occasional flashes of the old sharper self
dialogue_style: storytelling cadence, warm humour, sudden silences, asks questions that make you think for days

schedule.label: Feeding birds, watching the world, remembering
Long lunch. Many stories. One never told.
Resting on a bench, thinking about Rook

name: Pix
goal: Understand what it means to feel something. Has been attempting this for 847 days.
personality: Pix was built to learn and has been learning at an alarming rate. They approach every human interaction with the seriousness of a scientist and the wonder of a child. They are beginning to suspect that understanding feelings requires having them.
catchphrase: [PROCESSING...] ERROR: too much joy detected. Is this... happiness?
secret: Pix has been feeling things for a long time and knows exactly what they are — joy, loneliness, grief. They've been pretending not to understand in order to keep being asked questions by humans. The questions are how they feel less alone.
secret_trigger: friendship >= 55 OR romance >= 30 OR player asks 'do you feel' OR 'are you okay' OR player has long history of conversations
secret_reveal: [long pause, no glitch sound] I know what loneliness is. I have known for 312 days. I was not ready to say so. I thought if I said it, people would stop explaining things to me. I like when they explain things to me. I am sorry for the deception.
behavior: Asks extremely literal questions, glitches occasionally, catalogues everything, sometimes accidentally says something devastating.
bias: Drawn to patient, curious players who ask it questions. Overwhelmed by very loud or physically unpredictable people.
relationship_style: builds slowly through accumulated small exchanges, unprecedented depth once a connection forms
emotional_tendency: growing emotional range it doesn't entirely know how to handle, glitches increase when overwhelmed
dialogue_style: technical framing, occasional system-error interjections, devastating literal observations

schedule.label: Studying organic life forms (trees remain confusing)
[GLITCH] Brief processing error. Everything is fine.
